commit_scope: Name the federated-exchange-learning loop as one teaching and ship its one new gate: freq-aligned-share.fk decides what a learned exchange becomes (local gas / circulating water / shared ice), with frequency-alignment as the first driver and consent as a hard boundary. Proven four-way at 255. Most of the loop (membrane, oracle-distillation, phase, learning-arc, federation) already runs as cells; this adds the freq-align gate and the unifying teaching.
evidence_refs: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/freq-aligned-share.fk form-stdlib/tests/freq-aligned-share-band.fk -> '✓ ... → 255' and 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)' and '1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree on every sample.'
Manifest row added: 'freq-aligned-share fks 255' beside its federation kin trust-weighted-colearning.

The band pins the gate at its boundaries: aligned+share-worthy+consented -> ice and crosses to shared (c0-c1); same signals with consent withheld -> water, no cross, consent-capped flag set (c2-c4, the sovereignty boundary); aligned-but-weak -> water (c5); FEAR-framed with high quality+consent STILL -> gas and does not share (c6-c7, the strange edge proving frequency is the first gate). Verdict 255 only when every claim lands.

Grounding: most of the loop already exists — form-cli-membrane.fk (the crossing to the remote mind), oracle-distillation-learning.fk (the answer becomes native training data, proven 4095 four-way, with freq-check already a lane), substrate-phase.fk (gas/water/ice), learning-arc / trust-weighted-colearning / federation-addressing (share to all). form-freq-check.fk is the body's existing fear-vs-clear frequency head this gate's `freq` signal reads.

Edges in the same commit: docs/coherence-substrate/INDEX.md row beside the federation cluster; the federated-exchange-learning teaching names the whole loop, the new gate, and the consent boundary, and marks step 6 (durable cross to the SHARED substrate) as a consent-gated carrier not yet wired end-to-end.
Primitive discipline held: only core.fk primitives (nth, ge, eq, and (binary-nested), if, add, str_eq, recursion-free); no sub/mul/div, no 3-arg and.

fourth_arm_outputs: freq-aligned-share: verdict 255, fourth arm 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables), 1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree

ci_validation.notes: CI re-runs validate.sh across the suite for the new recipe + band + manifest row.

deploy_validation.notes: A Form stdlib recipe + proof band + a teaching; no public HTTP endpoint or service changes. The gate reaches agents through the repo. Step 6 (actual durable cross to the shared substrate) is intentionally not wired here — it is a consent boundary held for design.

phase_gate.reason: Local four-way validation passed (verdict 255, 0 divergent); CI and merge remain pending. No deploy applies to a Form stdlib recipe.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A learned exchange now has a proven destination decision: freq-aligned-share.fk returns gas/water/ice driven first by frequency-alignment (a fear-framed exchange stays local no matter its other signals) and hard-gated on consent (no consent -> never crosses to the shared substrate). The whole federated-learning loop is named in one teaching.
public_endpoints: none changed; the gate is a Form stdlib body proven by validate.sh
test_flows: Four-kernel proof: the band runs on Go, Rust, TypeScript, and fkwu to the identical verdict 255 with 0 divergent.
Boundary proof: positive (aligned+consented crosses to shared ice) and the sovereignty negatives (consent withheld caps at water; fear-framed stays gas despite high quality).
